_meta.hardware: Snapdragon X Elite CRD (Adreno X1-85 / QNN GPU EP)
_meta.epistemics_warning: ⚠️ All findings are hypotheses derived from 1 model on 1 device. Confidence levels reflect mechanism understanding, not universal applicability. GPU EP behavior varies significantly by model architecture and Adreno driver version.
_meta.verification_methodology: Perf gains are only trusted after a dedicated ROUND-ROBIN interleaved A/B (>=10 rounds, paired per-round deltas) confirms them. Sequential per-hypothesis benching inflates gains ~2x via thermal/DVFS/contention drift; op-count is ground truth for whether a fusion fired.
_meta.recipe_sweep_2026_06_25_conclusion: All 5 recipe models -> BASELINE_IS_BEST on QNN GPU: no config beat the analyzer default baseline under interleaved verification. Notable: clip_text_model BUILDS on QNN GPU (it fails to build on QNN NPU) — a cross-device capability datapoint.

reliability_legend.verified: confirmed mechanism AND/OR a dedicated interleaved A/B / reproducible hard failure
reliability_legend.supported: mechanism confirmed but narrow (single architecture / not A/B-verified)
reliability_legend.preliminary: single experiment or sequential-only perf claim; needs interleaved A/B

findings.title: W8A8 QDQ hangs indefinitely on QNN GPU EP
findings.observation: Passing a W8A8 QDQ-annotated ONNX to QNN GPU EP causes infinite hang. winml build's _patch_device() sets quant=null for GPU, preventing this in normal user path.
findings.mechanism_hypothesis: QNN SDK's GPU EP does not support QDQ-quantized graphs. This is a known QNN SDK limitation. winml build already protects against this via _patch_device().
findings.action_for_autoconfig: Skip ALL quantization experiments for QNN GPU EP. Do not even attempt W8A8 or W8A16.
findings.confidence: high — hang confirmed; protection mechanism in _patch_device() confirmed by code inspection

findings.title: gelu_fusion improves latency STABILITY (p90/std) on QNN GPU, not p50
findings.observation: Raw export (287 nodes, unfused Gelu): p50=17.4ms, p90=29.2ms, std=5.90. Autoconf (251 nodes, fused Gelu): p50=17.7ms, p90=19.7ms, std=0.97. p50 nearly identical, p90 -48%, std -6x.
findings.mechanism_hypothesis: 5 separate GPU kernel dispatches (Mul→Div→Erf→Mul→Add) for unfused GELU create scheduling jitter. Single Gelu kernel eliminates dispatch overhead → dramatically lower tail latency.
findings.action_for_autoconfig: Always apply gelu_fusion for QNN GPU (stability benefit). Do not expect p50 improvement.
findings.confidence: high — mechanism is well-understood (GPU kernel dispatch overhead)
findings.scope: Any model with GELU activations on QNN GPU

findings.title: opset 21 on QNN GPU is neutral-to-negative — CONFIRMED across 7 models
findings.observation: catalog_gpu_sweep.py full sweep 2026-06-18 (8 models, 13 hypotheses, 3x300 iters + Phase C confirmation): opset21 gains: DINOv2-small +1.22% (MARGINAL), ResNet-18 +3.27% (MARGINAL), MobileViT -3.42% (DISCARD), roberta-squad2 -1.14% (DISCARD), tinyroberta -2.68% (DISCARD), rad-dino -2.63% (DISCARD), bge-small +0.16% (DISCARD). Range: -5.42% to +3.27%. No model shows meaningful opset21 gain on GPU. Opposite of QNN NPU behavior (DINOv2 +30.6% on NPU).
findings.mechanism_hypothesis: QNN GPU EP does not have architecture-specific optimizations that benefit from opset21 graph differences (unlike NPU which shows DINOv2-specific speedup). GPU shader compilation is independent of ONNX opset semantics.
findings.action_for_autoconfig: Do NOT try opset 19 or opset 21 for QNN GPU EP. Default to opset 17. Rule is now confirmed across 7 models.
findings.reverification_2026_06_25: RECONFIRMED. 5-model recipe sweep (swinv2 opset21 DISCARD, koelectra DISCARD, mpnet MARGINAL/noise, clip opset21 -65% DISCARD, segformer KEEP-but-unconfirmed) plus a dedicated 10-round interleaved A/B on segformer: h3 (opset21) median 87.42ms vs h0 85.38ms = -2.39%, faster 5/10 rounds -> NOISE_NO_GAIN. The +8.2% segformer opset21 seen in sequential phase-B benching was pure time-drift; it vanishes under interleaving. opset 17 remains the GPU default.

findings.title: opset21 + matmul_transpose_fusion do NOT transfer from QNN NPU to GPU — verified
findings.observation: The verified swinv2 QNN NPU win (npu-014: opset21 + matmul_transpose_fusion = +12.7%, 10/10 interleaved rounds) REVERSES on QNN GPU. 5-model recipe sweep 2026-06-25: swinv2 GPU h4/h9/h10 (matmul_transpose variants) all DISCARD (~26% slower, ~72ms vs 57.5ms baseline); opset21 (h3) DISCARD. matmul_transpose_fusion is a NO-OP on the pure-transformer recipe models (mpnet, koelectra — the standalone MatMul+Transpose pattern is absent). Dedicated 10-round segformer GPU A/B independently confirms opset21 = -2.39% (NOISE_NO_GAIN).
findings.mechanism_hypothesis: FusedMatMul / opset21 graph changes that the QNN NPU (HTP) backend exploits do not map to a faster Adreno GPU shader schedule; on GPU the extra fusion either does not fire (no matching pattern) or adds dispatch overhead. Fusion benefit is architecture-AND-device-specific.
findings.action_for_autoconfig: Never assume an EP/device-specific fusion win transfers across devices. Do NOT apply matmul_transpose_fusion or opset21 as defaults for QNN GPU. Re-verify any cross-device perf claim with a dedicated interleaved A/B.
findings.confidence: high — 5-model recipe sweep + dedicated 10-round segformer interleaved A/B
findings.scope: QNN GPU (Adreno X1-85). Contrast with npu-014 (same flags help swinv2 on NPU).

findings.title: skip_layer_norm_fusion is a QNN GPU latency hazard (2-4x regression) — verified
findings.observation: 5-model recipe GPU sweep 2026-06-25: enabling skip_layer_norm_fusion (h8) produced large, consistent regressions across three transformer architectures — koelectra ~3-4x, segformer ~193ms (vs ~117ms baseline), mpnet 140-225ms (vs ~108ms baseline, up to ~2x). The fusion fires (graph changes) but the resulting fused op is slow on the Adreno GPU shader path.
findings.mechanism_hypothesis: The SkipLayerNormalization fused op does not have an efficient Adreno GPU kernel under the QNN GPU EP; the fused path is slower than the unfused Add + LayerNormalization sequence it replaces.
findings.action_for_autoconfig: Hard-skip skip_layer_norm_fusion for QNN GPU EP across all architectures.
findings.confidence: high — consistent large regression across 3 architectures
findings.scope: QNN GPU EP (Adreno X1-85), any transformer with LayerNorm

findings.title: analyzer default auto-fusions are perf-neutral on QNN GPU; --no-analyze yields no gain — verified
findings.observation: Across the recipe GPU sweep, --no-analyze (h13, which drops the analyzer's auto-injected gelu_fusion + matmul_add_fusion) repeatedly looked like a winner in sequential phase-B benching (segformer +22.6%, mpnet +3.62%) but every such gain washed out. Dedicated 10-round segformer interleaved A/B: h13 (--no-analyze) median 85.39ms vs h0 (analyzer on) 85.38ms = -0.01%, faster 5/10 rounds -> NOISE_NO_GAIN. The analyzer's default fusions are perf-neutral for p50 here (and gpu-005 shows gelu_fusion helps tail stability), so the defaults never net-hurt.
findings.mechanism_hypothesis: gelu_fusion + matmul_add_fusion change op counts but produce equivalent GPU shader dispatch cost on Adreno for these models; neither helps nor hurts median latency. The large sequential 'gains' were cold-baseline time-drift, not a real effect of disabling the analyzer.
findings.action_for_autoconfig: Keep the analyzer ON by default for QNN GPU. Do not chase --no-analyze as an optimization; verify any apparent --no-analyze gain with an interleaved A/B before acting.

findings.title: FP32 baseline is already optimal for ConvNext on QNN GPU — no optimization pass helps
findings.observation: Full sweep of 11 passes/combinations on ConvNext QNN GPU: all returned 0% node reduction or worse latency. Baseline p50=17.7ms, p90=19.7ms, std=0.97.
findings.mechanism_hypothesis: 251/0/0/0 (all ops native on GPU, zero CPU fallback). ConvNext linear layers use Reshape→MatMul→Reshape, not bare MatMul+Add — so MatMulAdd→Conv2D rewrites don't match. autoconf (gelu_fusion + matmul_add_fusion) already applied all applicable transforms.
findings.action_for_autoconfig: Skip all graph optimization experiments for QNN GPU on ConvNext-class models. Use FP32 baseline directly.
findings.confidence: high — confirmed by 0% node delta on all rewrites + 251/0/0/0 analyze output
findings.do_not_generalize_to: Transformer models with bare MatMul+Add (those may benefit from rewrites)

findings.title: highdimRTR_lowdimRTR causes -6.9% regression on MobileViT QNN GPU — same root cause as npu-010
findings.scope: Models with Gemm->Reshape->Transpose hybrid unfold patterns (MobileViT). DINOv2 was not tested with highdimRTR on GPU separately.
findings.observation: catalog_gpu_sweep.py 2026-06-18: MobileViT h9 (opset21+matmul_transpose+attention_fusion bundle) p50=19.224ms vs baseline 17.985ms = -6.89% (DISCARD). Root cause analysis via ONNX diff on NPU version shows +36 extra Reshape nodes (same issue as npu-010). GPU regression is less severe than NPU (-6.9% vs -19%) due to lower DMA sensitivity on Adreno vs Hexagon HTP.
findings.mechanism_detail: Same as npu-010: highdimRTR inserts spurious Reshape pairs after Gemm in MobileViT hybrid unfold mechanism. Breaks Gemm+Reshape dispatch merging. Less severe on GPU than NPU.
findings.cross_ep_note: npu-010 and gpu-008 share the same root cause. Fix is the same: block highdimRTR for Gemm->Reshape->Transpose models.
findings.autoconfig_action: Same as npu-010: hard-block highdimRTR for models with Gemm->Reshape->Transpose patterns. analyze_insight.py skip_set hint required.
findings.source: catalog_gpu_sweep.py h0-h12 full sweep + npu-010 ONNX diff

findings.title: NHWC transformer hurts QNN GPU on Adreno X1-85 (~10% worse)
findings.observation: NHWC transformer: p50=19.5ms (+10%), p90=23.8ms (+21%), std=3.43 (3.5x worse). Consistent across multiple runs.
findings.mechanism_hypothesis: Adreno X1-85 + QNN GPU EP does not benefit from explicit NHWC layout transforms. QNN GPU EP handles layout internally; forcing NHWC via ORT creates additional Reshape overhead without the memory alignment benefit.
findings.action_for_autoconfig: Do NOT apply nhwc-transformer for QNN GPU EP.
findings.confidence: medium — observed consistently; mechanism hypothesis, not confirmed
findings.do_not_generalize_to: Non-Adreno GPUs (NVIDIA, Intel Arc) — NHWC may help there

findings.title: winml compile appears to hurt QNN GPU (~34% regression) — SINGLE EXPERIMENT, LOW CONFIDENCE
findings.observation: FP32 + compile: p50=23.7ms vs baseline 17.7ms (+34%). Single experiment only.
findings.mechanism_hypothesis: QNN GPU EP compile (EPContext) is designed for NPU (HTP). On GPU EP, the compilation path may force a different dispatch mode that bypasses the optimized GPU shader path. QNN SDK likely has a GPU-specific compilation flow that winml compile doesn't trigger correctly.
findings.action_for_autoconfig: AVOID winml compile for QNN GPU EP. Direction (regression) is consistent with mechanism hypothesis and 34% is a large signal, but this is a single experiment. Until replicated, treat as likely harmful but not confirmed.
findings.confidence: low — single experiment. 34% gap is above DVFS noise level (CV ~0.05 → noise ~1ms, gap is 6ms). Direction probably real but magnitude uncertain.
findings.do_not_generalize_to: QNN NPU EP (compile always helps NPU)

findings.title: transpose_optimizer p50 gain on QNN GPU is UNVERIFIED — sequential-only, no-ops on recipe transformers
findings.confidence: low — the +8-17% p50 numbers are from sequential phase benching that this session has repeatedly shown inflates gains ~2x; never confirmed by a dedicated interleaved A/B. DINOv2 +16.67% had an in-sweep confirm but no paired A/B; ResNet +8.38% was MARGINAL_UNCONFIRMED.
findings.scope: Conv-dominant (ResNet) and ViT-class (DINOv2) models on QNN GPU. Likely architecture-general for models with Transpose-heavy graphs.
findings.observation: catalog_gpu_sweep.py sweep 2026-06-18: h12 (transpose_optimizer) KEEP_CONFIRMED. DINOv2-small: p50 26.372ms -> 21.977ms = +16.67% (all 5 sessions passed, Phase C confirmed). ResNet-18: p50 6.823ms -> 6.251ms = +8.38% (MARGINAL_UNCONFIRMED — Phase C did not confirm, needs more sessions). NLP models: neutral or BUILD_FAIL. rad-dino: +1.33% (MARGINAL). gelu_fusion explicit (h11) also KEEP_CONFIRMED on DINOv2: +13.86%.
findings.mechanism_hypothesis: transpose_optimizer eliminates redundant Transpose(NCHW->NHWC->NCHW) pairs around Conv/pooling in the graph. QNN GPU EP benefits from fewer Transpose ops because each requires a memory layout pass on Adreno. For DINOv2 and ResNet, the optimizer removes enough Transposes to provide meaningful latency reduction.
no_benefit_models: NLP models — most failed to build with transpose_optimizer; likely due to IR version incompatibility
findings.autoconfig_action: Apply transpose_optimizer as default for QNN GPU EP on Conv+ViT models. AVOID for NLP models until BUILD_FAIL issue is resolved. Feature gap: diagnose why h12 causes BUILD_FAIL on BERT/RoBERTa models.
findings.reverification_2026_06_25: On the 5-model recipe GPU sweep, h12 (transpose_optimizer) was a NO-OP (built graph byte-identical to baseline, bench skipped) on swinv2, mpnet and koelectra — the pass does not even fire on these transformer architectures. Treat the Conv/ViT p50 gain as a hypothesis requiring a dedicated interleaved A/B (like the swinv2 NPU npu-014 / segformer GPU protocol) before it is trusted or applied as a default.

opset.rationale: gpu-006 CONFIRMED: opset 21 neutral-to-negative across 7 models. Stay at opset 17.
opset.dialectical_note: ⚠️ May change once opset 21 GPU experiment is run.

skip: all — QDQ hangs on GPU EP (gpu-004)
quantization.dialectical_note: ⚠️ This is a QNN SDK limitation, not winml. May change with future QNN SDK versions that support GPU quantization.

compile.dialectical_note: ⚠️ gpu-003: compile regresses QNN GPU. Confirmed by single experiment. Re-validate if winml compile behavior changes.

graph_passes.recommended: autoconf defaults + transpose_optimizer for Conv/ViT models
graph_passes.dialectical_note: ⚠️ matmul_transpose_fusion / opset21 help swinv2 on NPU (npu-014) but DISCARD on GPU (gpu-009) — fusion wins are device-specific. skip_layer_norm_fusion is a verified GPU hazard (gpu-010). transpose_optimizer p50 gain (gpu-007) is unverified — needs a dedicated interleaved A/B before use.
